content: Which of the following statements about meiosis in sexually reproducing plants is INCORRECT?

options.content: The end products of meiosis II are haploid gametes.
The four products of meiosis are genetically different.
Meiotic recombination takes place in both males and females
In most flowering plants, only one of the four products of meiosis survives in females.

explanation:
The incorrect statement is Option A.

Explanation:

Option A: “The end products of meiosis II are haploid gametes.”

In plants, meiosis produces haploid spores (microspores in males, megaspores in females), not gametes.

Gametes (sperm and egg) arise later by mitotic division within the haploid gametophyte.

Option B: “The four products of meiosis are genetically different.”

True, due to independent assortment and crossing-over.

Option C: “Meiotic recombination takes place in both males and females.”

True, recombination occurs during meiosis in both sexes.

Option D: “In most flowering plants, only one of the four products of meiosis survives in females.”

True, only one megaspore survives; the other three degenerate.

content: A mitotic drug inhibits microtubule formation. Which one of the following stages of karyokinesis will be the first to get affected by the drug?
explanation: During metaphase, chromosomes align at the metaphase plate, which is centrally located within the cell. This precise alignment is facilitated by the mitotic spindle, composed of microtubules. If a drug inhibits the formation of microtubules, it disrupts the formation of the spindle fibers necessary for aligning chromosomes correctly at the metaphase plate. Therefore, the inhibition of microtubule formation would first impact the metaphase stage of karyokinesis.

content: Let ' $X$ ' be the perpendicular distance from the centromere of each chromosome to the equatorial plane of a human cell. Which of the following stages of the cell cycle is most likely to have the HIGHEST average value of $X^{\prime}$ ?
explanation: During late anaphase, the chromosomes are moving towards the poles of the cell. The centromeres are further from the equatorial plane compared to metaphase or early anaphase, as the chromatids are being pulled apart towards the poles.

content:
$$ \text { Match the human cell types or (column I) to the number of chromatids (column II) } $$

Column I Column II
(a) Col$$G_2 \text { phase cells }
$$		 (i) 46
(b) $$
G_1 \text { phase cells }
$$		 (ii) 92
(c) Spermatogonia (pre-replication)
 (iii) 23
(d) Ovum

options.content: $a-i i, b-i, c-i, d-i i i$

explanation: DNA replication occurs in the $S$ phase, due to which the chromosome number doubles from $46(2 n)$ to $92(4 n)$ in the $S$ and $G_2$ phases. After mitosis, the cell returns to its $2 n$ state, hence the $\mathrm{G}_1$ phase has $\mathbf{4 6}$ chromosomes. Spermatogonia before replication are diploid and have $\mathbf{4 6}$ chromosomes. They later divide to form haploid spermatocytes. Ova are haploid as they are gametes, and hence have $\mathbf{2 3}$ chromosomes.
